# Feature Specification

> Copy this template and fill in each section. Remove the instructional comments (lines starting with `>`) before submitting.

## Title
<!-- One-line description of the feature -->

## Context
<!-- Why does this feature matter? What user problem does it solve? -->

## Requirements

### Functional
<!-- What must the feature do? Use checkboxes for each requirement. -->
- [ ] Requirement 1
- [ ] Requirement 2

### Non-Functional
<!-- Performance, accessibility, i18n, security constraints -->
- [ ] Must work in all 9 supported languages
- [ ] Must be accessible (WCAG 2.0 AA)
- [ ] Must work in dark mode

## Technical Design

### Affected Files
<!-- List every file that needs to change. This helps agents scope their work. -->
| File | Change Type | Description |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| `src/components/Example.tsx` | New | New component |
| `src/lib/translations/en.ts` | Modified | Add translation keys |

### API Changes
<!-- New or modified endpoints, request/response schemas -->

### Database Changes
<!-- New fields, indexes, queries -->

## Acceptance Criteria
<!-- How do we know this is done? These become test cases. -->
- [ ] Given [context], when [action], then [result]
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated
- [ ] E2E test covers the happy path

## Out of Scope
<!-- What this spec intentionally does NOT cover -->

## Dependencies
<!-- Other specs, PRs, or external services required -->
